mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or positive return frequency drops below 52% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 55% of triggered instances over a 24-month backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and builder confidence, driving sustained positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce a positive return frequency above 54% over 60-day windows in a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output that supports corporate earnings growth, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days specifically during periods of stable inflation.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 when CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.4%, or returns turn negative during industrial expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.35% monthly threshold forces markets to price in imminent monetary tightening, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of high-inflation trigger months over a 36-month evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market absorption, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields a negative return frequency above 55% during periods of simultaneous payroll growth and unemployment decline.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5% annualized confirms strong aggregate demand that translates into higher corporate profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days during low-volatility environments.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if VIX exceeds 25 and GDP growth remains above 2.5% while 20-day forward returns are negative in > 58% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production directly correlate with higher factory utilization and output, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between INDPRO growth and 5-day forward returns falls below 0.02 across all rolling 24-month windows.
